I have a Blazor Web App with Interactive Server (per-page).

I'm building a company intranet. I need ASP.NET Core Identity with
role-based access control. Here's what I need:

1. Set up Identity with three roles: Admin, Manager, and Employee.
   Use an in-memory database for now. Seed one test user per role
   on startup so I can test everything:
   - admin@company.com (Admin role)
   - manager@company.com (Manager role)
   - employee@company.com (Employee role)
   All with password "Test123!"

2. An interactive /admin/users page. Only Admins should be able to
   access this page. Show a list of all users with their roles
   (query from UserManager).

3. An interactive /reports page that both Admins and Managers can access
   but not regular Employees. Show some placeholder report content.

4. An interactive /directory page accessible to any authenticated user.
   Show a simple employee list.

5. The navigation menu should show or hide links based on the current
   user's roles — don't show the admin link to non-admins, don't show
   reports to employees.

6. If an unauthenticated user tries to visit any protected page, they
   should be sent to a login page. If an authenticated user visits a
   page they don't have the role for, they should see an "Access Denied"
   message.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good understanding of the existing codebase. Let me plan the implementation:

1. Add NuGet packages: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity.EntityFrameworkCore, Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.InMemory
2. Create ApplicationDbContext
3. Configure Identity in Program.cs with in-memory DB
4. Seed roles and users
5. Create Login page (non-interactive, uses HTTP POST for cookie auth)
6. Create AccessDenied page
7. Create /admin/users page (interactive server, Admin only)
8. Create /reports page (interactive server, Admin+Manager)


9. Create /directory page (interactive server, any authenticated)
10. Update NavMenu to show/hide based on roles
11. Update Routes.razor to use AuthorizeRouteView
12. Update App.razor to include CascadingAuthenticationState
13. Add logout functionality

The key thing to remember is that login and logout need to happen through HTTP with cookie authentication rather than interactive components, while protected pages use authorization attributes with role checks. I'll need to wrap the router with CascadingAuthenticationState and add the authentication middleware to make this all work together.

data.content:
Here's a summary of everything that was set up:

**Identity & Data**
- `Data/ApplicationDbContext.cs` — EF Core Identity context with in-memory DB
- `Program.cs` — Identity services, cookie config, role/user seeding (3 users with roles)

**Pages**
- `/account/login` — SSR login form (cookie auth)
- `/account/logout` — POST-based sign out
- `/account/access-denied` — "Access Denied" message
- `/admin/users` — Interactive, `[Authorize(Roles = "Admin")]`, lists all users+roles
- `/reports` — Interactive, `[Authorize(Roles = "Admin,Manager")]`, placeholder reports
- `/directory` — Interactive, `[Authorize]`, employee directory

**Navigation & Auth Flow**
- `NavMenu.razor` — Uses `<AuthorizeView>` with role checks to show/hide links
- `MainLayout.razor` — Top bar shows username + logout (or login link)
- `Routes.razor` — Uses `<AuthorizeRouteView>` to redirect unauthenticated users to login, and show Access Denied for unauthorized
- `RedirectToLogin.razor` — Handles the redirect with ReturnUrl

**Test credentials**: `admin@company.com`, `manager@company.com`, `employee@company.com` — all with password `Test123!`
